body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** commit 16a9c8b7e752cd0375e7770aa2b0447d3b7193b0 (PR #58341) by @RaviPidaparthi ## What Changed PR #58341 introduced two major behavioral changes to the Responses server: 1. **Eager pre-handler validation**: `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` are now validated against the provider *before* `CreateAsync` is invoked. Invalid references now return HTTP 404/400 immediately instead of being silently ignored. The resolved history items are cached so handlers that later call `GetHistoryAsync()` reuse the prefetched result. 2. **Structured error field propagation**: `ResourceNotFoundException` gained `Code` and `Param` properties (mirroring `BadRequestException`). `StorageErrorMapper` now extracts all four fields (`message`, `code`, `param`, `type`) from Foundry error JSON and propagates them through the exception hierarchy. Non-400/404 upstream error codes are normalized to HTTP 500 (never proxied). ## Gaps Found - **README.md — Troubleshooting section** does not mention that invalid `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` values are rejected with 400/404 before the handler runs (users expect this to be silent or handler-handled) - **README.md — Troubleshooting section** does not mention that non-400/404 errors from upstream Foundry storage are normalized to HTTP 500 - **docs/handler-implementation-guide.md — Handler Exceptions table** still shows `ResourceNotFoundException` → `error.code: null`, but it now carries `Code` and `Param` from the exception (matching `BadRequestException` behavior) - **docs/handler-implementation-guide.md — Validation Pipeline section** does not document the new pre-handler validation step for `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id`
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Edit Locate the **Troubleshooting** section (~line 183) and extend the common-errors list to include the new pre-handler validation errors and the 500-normalization behavior: ```markdown ## Troubleshooting ### Common errors - **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as `model` (when provided) are valid and that `input` items are well-formed. - **400 Bad Request** (`conversation.id`): The `conversation.id` field referenced a conversation that the provider rejected as invalid. This validation runs before your handler is invoked. - **404 Not Found** (`previous_response_id`): The `previous_response_id` field referenced a response that does not exist or has expired. This validation runs before your handler is invoked; the handler is never called when the reference is invalid. - **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL. - **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response was not created with `background=true`, or it has already reached a terminal state. - **404 Not Found** (cancel): The response was created with `store=false`, or a non-background response is still in-flight and not findable. - **500 Internal Server Error**: An unexpected error occurred server-side. Errors from the upstream Foundry storage layer that are neither 400 nor 404 are always returned as HTTP 500 — the original upstream status code is never proxied to clients. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Edit **Change 1 — Handler Exceptions table**: Update the `ResourceNotFoundException` row to reflect that `Code` and `Param` are now propagated (lines around 1134): Replace the existing table: ```markdown | Exception Type | HTTP Status | Response Status | `error.code` | `error.message` | |---------------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|----------------| | `BadRequestException` | 400 | `failed` | from exception (e.g., `"invalid_value"`) | from exception | | `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | `null` | from exception | | `ResponsesApiException` | from exception | `failed` | from exception | from exception | | Any other exception | 500 | `failed` | `"server_error"` | `"An internal error occurred."` | | `OperationCanceledException` | *(special)* | `cancelled` | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | ``` With: ```markdown | Exception Type | HTTP Status | Response Status | `error.code` | `error.param` | `error.message` | |---------------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|----------------| | `BadRequestException` | 400 | `failed` | from `Code` (e.g., `"invalid_value"`) | from `ParamName` | from exception | | `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | from `Code` | from `Param` | from exception | | `ResponsesApiException` | from exception | `failed` | from exception | from exception | from exception | | Any other exception | 500 | `failed` | `"server_error"` | `null` | `"An internal error occurred."` | | `OperationCanceledException` | *(special)* | `cancelled` | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | ``` **Change 2 — Validation Pipeline section**: Extend the section (~line ~1172) to document the new pre-handler validation: After the existing paragraph that begins _"Bad client input returns HTTP 400 before your handler runs"_, add: ```markdown **Pre-handler reference validation**: In addition to payload schema validation, the library validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` against the storage provider *before* `CreateAsync` is called: - An unrecognised `previous_response_id` causes the library to return HTTP 404 immediately; your handler is never invoked. - An invalid `conversation.id` causes the library to return HTTP 400 immediately; your handler is never invoked. When the references are valid the resolved history items are cached, so `GetHistoryAsync()` inside your handler reuses the prefetched result without issuing a second storage call. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are needed for this change — the additions are prose and a table update. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). Only the **Troubleshooting** section content needs updating. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no README diffs from snippet injection 4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [AgentServer] Docs: Update error handling docs for eager validation and structured error fields
